title: Google Drive access denied with 403 error
symptom: When attempting to download files from Google Drive in n8n, the user receives a 'Request failed with status code 403' error. This indicates a permissions issue with the Google Drive API.
not_this_if: Error occurs during other operations like listing folders
root_cause: The 403 error typically indicates that the application does not have the necessary permissions to access the requested resource. This can happen if the required Google Drive API scopes are not enabled in the Google Cloud Console.
fix_steps: Go to your Google Cloud Console.
Check the OAuth 2.0 consent screen and ensure all necessary Google Drive scopes are enabled.
If any scopes are missing, add them manually.
Re-authenticate your Google account in n8n to apply the changes.
agent_instruction: Ensure all required Google Drive API scopes are enabled in the Google Cloud Console and re-authenticate in n8n.
